# services.deluge

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.deluge`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.deluge.authFile` | `absolute path` | The file managing the authentication for deluge, the format of this file is straightforward, each line contains a username:password:level tuple in plaintext. It only has an effect when {option}`services.deluge.declarative` is set to `true`. See <https://dev.deluge-torrent.org/wiki/UserGuide/Authentication> for more information. |
| `services.deluge.config` | `attribute set` | Deluge core configuration for the core.conf file. Only has an effect when {option}`services.deluge.declarative` is set to `true`. String values must be quoted, integer and boolean values must not. See <https://git.deluge-torrent.org/deluge/tree/deluge/core/preferencesmanager.py#n41> for the available options. |
| `services.deluge.dataDir` | `absolute path` | The directory where deluge will create files. |
| `services.deluge.declarative` | `boolean` | Whether to use a declarative deluge configuration. Only if set to `true`, the options {option}`services.deluge.config`, {option}`services.deluge.openFirewall` and {option}`services.deluge.authFile` will be applied. |
| `services.deluge.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Deluge daemon. |
| `services.deluge.extraPackages` | `list of package` | Extra packages available at runtime to enable Deluge's plugins. For example, extraction utilities are required for the built-in "Extractor" plugin. This always contains unzip, gnutar, xz and bzip2. |
| `services.deluge.group` | `string` | Group under which deluge runs. |
| `services.deluge.openFilesLimit` | `signed integer or string` | Number of files to allow deluged to open. |
| `services.deluge.openFirewall` | `boolean` | Whether to open the firewall for the ports in {option}`services.deluge.config.listen_ports`. It only takes effet if {option}`services.deluge.declarative` is set to `true`. It does NOT apply to the daemon port nor the web UI port. To access those ports securely check the documentation <https://dev.deluge-torrent.org/wiki/UserGuide/ThinClient#CreateSSHTunnel> or use a VPN or configure certificates for deluge. |
| `services.deluge.package` | `package` | The deluge-2_x package to use. |
| `services.deluge.user` | `string` | User account under which deluge runs. |
| `services.deluge.web.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Deluge Web daemon. |
| `services.deluge.web.openFirewall` | `boolean` | Open ports in the firewall for deluge web daemon |
| `services.deluge.web.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Deluge web UI port. |
